title: narcissus pseudonarcissus var. stenanthus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus var. stenanthus, also known as the wild daffodil or Lent lily, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its white or pale yellow trumpet-shaped flowers and long, narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the wild daffodil are attributed to its ability to reduce anxiety, insomnia, and other nervous system disorders, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant medicinally.
